questions.q: In Hindu tradition, the avatar Varaha of Vishnu takes the form of which animal?
questions.explanation: Varaha is the boar avatar of Vishnu, his name meaning 'boar' in Sanskrit.

questions.q: Of which deity is Varaha considered an avatar (incarnation)?
questions.explanation: Varaha is counted among the dashavatara, the ten principal incarnations of Lord Vishnu.

questions.q: In the Varaha episode, what does the boar avatar rescue from the cosmic waters?
questions.explanation: Varaha dives into the cosmic ocean to rescue the earth, which had been dragged into the depths.

questions.q: In the Puranas, the earth that Varaha rescues is personified as which goddess?
questions.explanation: The earth is personified as the goddess Bhudevi (Bhumi), whom Varaha lifts from the waters.

questions.q: Which demon (asura) does Varaha slay in the Bhagavata Purana account?
questions.explanation: Varaha slays the asura Hiranyaksha, who had carried the earth down into the cosmic ocean.

questions.q: How does Varaha famously raise the earth back to its place?
questions.explanation: Varaha lifts the earth-goddess out of the waters upon his tusks.

questions.q: As which number among Vishnu's ten avatars is Varaha traditionally counted?
questions.explanation: Varaha is traditionally listed as the third of Vishnu's ten avatars (dashavatara).

questions.q: In the dashavatara sequence, which avatar of Vishnu immediately precedes Varaha the boar?
questions.explanation: Kurma the tortoise is the second avatar, coming just before Varaha, the third.

questions.q: Varaha's rescue of the earth chiefly takes place in which setting?
questions.explanation: The episode unfolds in the cosmic waters, into which the earth had been submerged.

questions.q: In the dashavatara list, which fierce avatar of Vishnu follows Varaha the boar?
questions.explanation: Narasimha, the man-lion, is the fourth avatar, following Varaha the boar.

questions.q: Which Purana gives the well-known account of Varaha slaying Hiranyaksha and lifting the earth?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana relates how Varaha slew Hiranyaksha and raised the earth upon his tusks.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the demon Hiranyaksha slain by Varaha had a brother who was later killed by another avatar. Who was that brother?
questions.explanation: Hiranyaksha's brother was Hiranyakashipu, later slain by Vishnu's Narasimha avatar.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, the demons Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu were born to the sage Kashyapa and which mother?
questions.explanation: Diti was the mother of the daitya brothers Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu, fathered by sage Kashyapa.

questions.q: In Puranic tradition, Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu, slain by Vishnu's avatars, are said to be a cursed incarnation of which of Vishnu's gatekeepers?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana identifies the demon brothers as Jaya and Vijaya, Vishnu's gatekeepers cursed to be born as asuras.

questions.q: In some Puranic accounts, from whose nostril does the tiny boar emerge before swelling to enormous size to rescue the earth?
questions.explanation: In several accounts the boar springs from the nostril of Brahma the creator, then grows to a colossal form.

questions.q: Which goddess, as Vishnu's consort, is identified with the earth-goddess Bhudevi whom Varaha rescues?
questions.explanation: Bhudevi is regarded as a form of Lakshmi, the consort of Vishnu, in the earth-goddess aspect.

questions.q: In classical iconography, Varaha is most often depicted as which type of figure?
options: A boar-headed man with a human body
questions.explanation: Varaha is commonly carved and painted with a boar's head atop a powerful human body, often holding Vishnu's emblems.

questions.q: Which discus, an emblem of Vishnu, is often shown in the hand of Varaha in his iconography?
questions.explanation: As an avatar of Vishnu, Varaha is frequently depicted bearing the Sudarshana Chakra, Vishnu's discus.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, how is the long conflict between Varaha and Hiranyaksha chiefly described?
options: A fierce duel beneath the waters
questions.explanation: The texts describe a prolonged underwater duel in which Varaha finally slays the demon Hiranyaksha.

questions.q: In iconography, the earth-goddess rescued by Varaha is typically shown how in relation to the boar?
options: Seated on a lotus far below
Resting upon his tusk or shoulder
questions.explanation: Varaha is classically depicted with the small figure of Bhudevi lifted upon his tusk or shoulder.

questions.q: The boar lifting the earth appears as a cosmic emblem in which early Vedic-Brahmanical text, where the earth is raised by a boar named Emusha?
questions.explanation: The Shatapatha Brahmana preserves an early version in which a boar called Emusha raises the earth from the waters.

questions.q: The Bhu Varaha Swamy temple, an important shrine dedicated to Vishnu's boar avatar Varaha, is located in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Bhu Varaha Swamy temple at Srimushnam in Tamil Nadu is a celebrated shrine devoted to Varaha, the boar avatar of Vishnu.

questions.q: In some Puranic and ritual traditions, Varaha is identified with the sacrifice itself and given which epithet, 'the sacrificial boar'?
questions.explanation: As Yajna Varaha, the boar is identified with the cosmic sacrifice, his limbs equated with elements of the Vedic rite.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, the curse that doomed the gatekeepers to be born as Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu was pronounced by which group of child-sages?
questions.explanation: The four boy-sages, the Kumaras (Sanaka and his brothers), cursed Jaya and Vijaya when barred from Vishnu's abode.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, the gatekeepers cursed to become demons were given a choice; for which option did they opt that brought a swifter return to Vishnu?
options: Seven lives as enemies rather than three as devotees
Three lives as Vishnu's enemies rather than seven as devotees
questions.explanation: They chose three births as Vishnu's foes, slain by his avatars, over seven gentler births, hastening their return.

questions.q: In the Puranic genealogy, the daityas Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu, opponents of Vishnu's avatars, are reckoned among the offspring of which patriarch sage?
questions.explanation: The sage Kashyapa, with his wife Diti, is named as the father of the daitya brothers in the Puranas.

questions.q: Which short Upanishad-style Purana is devoted to Vishnu and named after the boar avatar?
questions.explanation: The Varaha Purana is one of the eighteen Mahapuranas, framed largely as Varaha's teaching to the earth-goddess.

questions.q: At Khajuraho, a famous monumental sculpture depicts Varaha in which striking form?
options: A boar-headed man slaying a serpent
A boar riding the eagle Garuda
A colossal fully zoomorphic boar covered with rows of carved deities
A miniature boar within a lotus
questions.explanation: The Khajuraho Varaha is a large solid stone boar whose body is covered with hundreds of small carved divine figures.

questions.q: In Hindu cosmology, the Varaha avatar lends its name to the present 'kalpa' (day of Brahma). What is that age called?
questions.explanation: The current kalpa is called the Shveta-varaha Kalpa, named for Varaha's lifting of the earth at its dawn.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, the boon that made Hiranyaksha and his brother so formidable, leading to their deaths by Vishnu's avatars, was originally granted by which deity?
questions.explanation: The daitya brothers obtained their near-invincible boons through austerities rewarded by Brahma the creator.
